잠언 20:1.content: Wine is a mocker and beer is a brawler. Whoever is led astray by them is not wise.

잠언 20:2.content: The terror of a king is like the roaring of a lion. He who provokes him to anger forfeits his own life.

잠언 20:3.content: It is an honor for a man to keep aloof from strife, but every fool will be quarreling.

잠언 20:4.content: The sluggard will not plow by reason of the winter; therefore he shall beg in harvest, and have nothing.

잠언 20:5.content: Counsel in the heart of man is like deep water, but a man of understanding will draw it out.

잠언 20:6.content: Many men claim to be men of unfailing love, but who can find a faithful man?

잠언 20:7.content: A righteous man walks in integrity. Blessed are his children after him.

잠언 20:8.content: A king who sits on the throne of judgment scatters away all evil with his eyes.

잠언 20:9.content: Who can say, “I have made my heart pure. I am clean and without sin?”

잠언 20:10.content: Differing weights and differing measures, both of them alike are an abomination to Yahweh.

잠언 20:11.content: Even a child makes himself known by his doings, whether his work is pure, and whether it is right.

잠언 20:12.content: The hearing ear, and the seeing eye, Yahweh has made even both of them.

잠언 20:13.content: Don’t love sleep, lest you come to poverty. Open your eyes, and you shall be satisfied with bread.

잠언 20:14.content: “It’s no good, it’s no good,” says the buyer; but when he is gone his way, then he boasts.

잠언 20:15.content: There is gold and abundance of rubies, but the lips of knowledge are a rare jewel.

잠언 20:16.content: Take the garment of one who puts up collateral for a stranger; and hold him in pledge for a wayward woman.

잠언 20:17.content: Fraudulent food is sweet to a man, but afterwards his mouth is filled with gravel.

잠언 20:18.content: Plans are established by advice; by wise guidance you wage war!

잠언 20:19.content: He who goes about as a tale-bearer reveals secrets; therefore don’t keep company with him who opens wide his lips.

잠언 20:20.content: Whoever curses his father or his mother, his lamp shall be put out in blackness of darkness.

잠언 20:21.content: An inheritance quickly gained at the beginning, won’t be blessed in the end.

잠언 20:22.content: Don’t say, “I will pay back evil.” Wait for Yahweh, and he will save you.

잠언 20:23.content: Yahweh detests differing weights, and dishonest scales are not pleasing.

잠언 20:24.content: A man’s steps are from Yahweh; how then can man understand his way?

잠언 20:25.content: It is a snare to a man to make a rash dedication, then later to consider his vows.

잠언 20:26.content: A wise king winnows out the wicked, and drives the threshing wheel over them.

잠언 20:27.content: The spirit of man is Yahweh’s lamp, searching all his innermost parts.

잠언 20:28.content: Love and faithfulness keep the king safe. His throne is sustained by love.

잠언 20:29.content: The glory of young men is their strength. The splendor of old men is their gray hair.

잠언 20:30.content: Wounding blows cleanse away evil, and beatings purge the innermost parts.
